tools.name: list_users
tools.description:
List users in the organization.

        Retrieves a list of user objects following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 structure.
        Supports OData query parameters for filtering, sorting, pagination, and field selection.

        This endpoint corresponds to GET /users in Microsoft Graph API.

        OData Query Parameters:
          - _top: Optional. Number of items to return (1-999, default: 100)
          - _skip: Optional. Number of items to skip for pagination (≥0, default: 0)
          - _filter: Optional. OData filter expression with support for:
            * Comparison operators: eq, ne, gt, lt, ge, le
            * String functions: startswith(), endswith(), contains()
            * Logical operators: and, or
            * Examples:
              - "displayName eq 'Alice Johnson'"
              - "displayName eq 'Alice' or displayName eq 'Bob'"
              - "startswith(displayName,'A') and accountEnabled eq true"
          - _orderby: Optional. OData orderby expression (e.g., "displayName asc")
          - _select: Optional. Comma-separated list of properties to return
          - _count: Optional. Include total count in response (true/False)

        Default Properties Returned:
          - businessPhones, displayName, givenName, id, jobTitle, mail,
          - mobilePhone, officeLocation, preferredLanguage, surname, userPrincipalName

        Response Structure:
          - Returns list of users with Microsoft Graph API v1.0 format:
            * @odata.context: Metadata context URL
            * @odata.count: Total count (if _count=true)
            * @odata.nextLink: Next page URL (if more results available)
            * value: Array of user objects

        Authentication:
          - Requires x-teams-access-token header with valid access token
          - Token will be validated against user database

        Status Codes:
          - 200: Success - Users retrieved successfully
          - 400: Bad Request - Invalid query parameters
          - 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing access token
          - 500: Internal Server Error

_skip.description: Number of items to skip for pagination

_filter.description: OData filter expression. Supports: eq, ne, gt, lt, ge, le operators; startswith(), endswith(), contains() functions; and, or logical operators. Examples: "displayName eq 'Alice Johnson'", "displayName eq 'Alice' or displayName eq 'Bob'", "startswith(displayName,'A') and accountEnabled eq true"

_orderby.description: OData orderby expression (e.g., "displayName asc" or "createdDateTime desc")

_select.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return (e.g., "displayName,mail,jobTitle")

tools.name: create_user
tools.description:
Create a new user in the organization.

        Creates a new user following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 structure.
        This endpoint corresponds to POST /users in Microsoft Graph API.

        Request Body Requirements:
          - userPrincipalName: Required. User principal name in email format (max 320 characters)
          - displayName: Required. Display name of the user (max 256 characters)

        Optional Properties:
          - givenName: Given name/first name (max 128 characters)
          - surname: Surname/last name (max 128 characters)
          - jobTitle: Job title (max 128 characters)
          - mail: Primary email address (max 320 characters)
          - businessPhones: Array of business phone numbers
          - mobilePhone: Mobile phone number
          - officeLocation: Office location (max 128 characters)
          - preferredLanguage: Preferred language code (e.g., "en-US")

        Validation Rules:
          - userPrincipalName must be in email format (contain @)
          - userPrincipalName must be unique within the organization
          - displayName cannot be empty or whitespace only
          - All string fields will be trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace

        Response Structure:
          - Returns created user with Microsoft Graph API v1.0 format:
            * @odata.context: Metadata context URL
            * id: Unique user identifier (UUID)
            * userPrincipalName, displayName, and all provided properties
            * createdDateTime: Creation timestamp
            * Organizational metadata (orgId)

        Authentication:
          - Requires x-teams-access-token header with valid access token
          - Token will be validated against user database

        Status Codes:
          - 201: Created - User created successfully
          - 400: Bad Request - Invalid user data or validation errors
          - 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing access token
          - 409: Conflict - User with same userPrincipalName already exists
          - 500: Internal Server Error

userPrincipalName.description: User principal name in email format (e.g., john.doe@contoso.com)

employeeType.description: Worker type (e.g., Employee, Contractor, Consultant, Vendor)

otherMails.description: Additional email addresses (max 250 entries, 250 chars each)

tools.name: get_user
tools.description:
Retrieve a specific user by their ID or userPrincipalName.

        Returns user metadata following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 structure.
        This endpoint corresponds to GET /users/{id | userPrincipalName} in Microsoft Graph API.

        Request Parameters (at least one required):
          - userId: Optional. User identifier (UUID format)
          - userPrincipalName: Optional. User principal name (email format)

        Parameter Behavior:
          - If only 'userId' provided: Lookup user by ID
          - If only 'userPrincipalName' provided: Lookup user by UPN
          - If both provided: Validates they reference the same user, returns error if mismatch

        Optional Query Parameters:
          - _select: Comma-separated list of properties to return

        Supported ID Formats:
          - User ID (UUID): e.g., "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012"
          - userPrincipalName: e.g., "john.doe@contoso.com"

        Response Structure:
          - Returns user with Microsoft Graph API v1.0 format:
            * @odata.context: Metadata context URL
            * id: Unique user identifier
            * userPrincipalName: User principal name
            * displayName: Display name
            * All available user properties (givenName, surname, jobTitle, mail, etc.)
            * createdDateTime: Creation timestamp
            * lastModifiedDateTime: Last modification timestamp

        Authentication:
          - Requires x-teams-access-token header with valid access token
          - Token will be validated against user database

        Status Codes:
          - 200: Success - User retrieved successfully
          - 400: Bad Request - Invalid parameters or mismatch when both provided
          - 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing access token
          - 404: Not Found - User not found
          - 500: Internal Server Error

userId.description: User identifier (UUID format). Either 'userId' or 'userPrincipalName' must be provided.

userPrincipalName.description: User principal name in email format (e.g., 'alice.manager@techcorp.com'). Either 'userId' or 'userPrincipalName' must be provided.

_select.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return (e.g., "displayName,mail,jobTitle")

tools.name: update_user
tools.description:
Update user properties.

        Partially updates user metadata following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 structure.
        Only provided fields will be updated, others remain unchanged.
        This endpoint corresponds to PATCH /users/{id | userPrincipalName} in Microsoft Graph API.

        Request Parameters (at least one required):
          - userId: Optional. User identifier (UUID format)
          - userPrincipalName: Optional. User principal name (email format)
          
        Parameter Behavior:
          - If only 'userId' provided: Update user by ID
          - If only 'userPrincipalName' provided: Update user by UPN
          - If both provided: Validates they reference the same user, returns error if mismatch

        Updatable Properties (all optional):
          - displayName: Display name (max 256 characters)
          - givenName: Given name/first name (max 128 characters)
          - surname: Surname/last name (max 128 characters)
          - jobTitle: Job title (max 128 characters)
          - mail: Primary email address (max 320 characters)
          - businessPhones: Array of business phone numbers
          - mobilePhone: Mobile phone number
          - officeLocation: Office location (max 128 characters)
          - preferredLanguage: Preferred language code

        Update Rules:
          - At least one field must be provided for update
          - userPrincipalName cannot be updated (immutable)
          - Null values will clear optional fields
          - Empty strings will be treated as null for clearing
          - All string fields will be trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace

        Response Structure:
          - Returns updated user with Microsoft Graph API v1.0 format
          - Includes lastModifiedDateTime with current timestamp

        Authentication:
          - Requires x-teams-access-token header with valid access token
          - Token will be validated against user database

        Status Codes:
          - 200: Success - User updated successfully
          - 400: Bad Request - No fields provided or invalid data
          - 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing access token
          - 404: Not Found - User not found
          - 500: Internal Server Error

userId.description: User identifier - accepts either user ID (UUID) or userPrincipalName (e.g., 'alice_manager' or 'alice.manager@techcorp.com')

userPrincipalName.description: User principal name in email format (e.g., 'alice.manager@techcorp.com'). Can be provided along with userId for validation.

tools.name: delete_user
tools.description:
Delete a user by ID or userPrincipalName.

        Deletes a user following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 behavior.
        By default, performs soft delete (user marked as deleted but preserved).
        This endpoint corresponds to DELETE /users/{id | userPrincipalName} in Microsoft Graph API.

        Request Parameters (at least one required):
          - userId: Optional. User identifier (UUID format)
          - userPrincipalName: Optional. User principal name (email format)

        Parameter Behavior:
          - If only 'userId' provided: Delete user by ID
          - If only 'userPrincipalName' provided: Delete user by UPN
          - If both provided: Validates they reference the same user, returns error if mismatch

        Delete Behavior:
          - Soft Delete (default): User is marked as deleted but data is preserved
          - User will no longer appear in regular queries or authentication
          - Soft-deleted users can potentially be restored
          - Associated data (chats, etc.) is preserved but inaccessible

        Cascade Effects:
          - User access is immediately revoked
          - User will not appear in user lists or searches
          - Associated organizational relationships are updated

        Response:
          - Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion
          - No response body as per Microsoft Graph API v1.0

        Authentication:
          - Requires x-teams-access-token header with valid access token
          - Token will be validated against user database

        Status Codes:
          - 204: No Content - User deleted successfully
          - 400: Bad Request - Invalid parameters or mismatch when both provided
          - 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing access token
          - 404: Not Found - User not found or already deleted
          - 500: Internal Server Error

userId.description: User identifier (UUID format). Either 'userId' or 'userPrincipalName' must be provided.

userPrincipalName.description: User principal name in email format (e.g., 'alice.manager@techcorp.com'). Either 'userId' or 'userPrincipalName' must be provided.

tools.name: create_chat
tools.description:
Create a new chat (one-on-one or group) following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 specification.

    API Endpoint: POST /chats

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions:
    - Delegated (work or school account): Chat.Create (least privileged), Chat.ReadWrite (higher privileged)
    - Delegated (personal Microsoft account): Not supported
    - Application: Chat.Create (least privileged)

    Important Notes:
    - Only one one-on-one chat can exist between two members. If a one-on-one chat already exists, this operation returns the existing chat and doesn't create a new one.
    - For delegated permissions, the authenticated user MUST be included in the members list
    - For application permissions, no membership requirement
    - This API does NOT require user_id parameter as it operates directly on the /chats resource

    Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/chat-post

chatType.description: Type of chat to create: 'oneOnOne' or 'group'

topic.description: Chat topic/title. Optional for oneOnOne (must be null), required for group chats. Maximum 255 characters.

members.description: List of conversation members. Each member must include _odata_type, roles, and user_odata_bind. Minimum 2 members required.

roles.description: Roles assigned to the member. In-tenant guest users must be assigned 'guest' role. Out-of-tenant external users must be assigned 'owner' role.

user_odata_bind.description: URL to user resource. Format: 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users('user-id')' or 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users('user-principal-name')'

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for federated users (external users from another organization)

installedApps.description: Apps to install in the chat during creation (optional)

teamsApp_odata_bind.description: URL to teams app resource. Format: 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/appCatalogs/teamsApps/app-id'

consentedPermissionSet.description: Consented permission set with RSC (Resource-Specific Consent) permissions (optional)

tools.name: update_chat
tools.description:
Update a chat's properties (currently only topic is supported). Only group chats can be updated.

    API Endpoint: PATCH /chats/{chat-id}

    This API does NOT require user_id parameter as it operates directly on the chat resource.

    Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/chat-patch

tools.name: list_chats
tools.description:
List chats for the authenticated user (Microsoft Graph API compliant).

    API Endpoint: GET /chats

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions:
    - Delegated (work or school account): Chat.Read (least privileged), Chat.ReadWrite (higher privileged)
    - Delegated (personal Microsoft account): Not supported
    - Application: Chat.Read.All (least privileged), Chat.ReadWrite.All (higher privileged)

    Microsoft Graph API Query Parameters (EXACT from documentation):

    Name: $expand
    Description: Currently supports members and lastMessagePreview properties.
    
    Name: $top
    Description: Controls the number of items per response. Maximum allowed $top value is 50.
    
    Name: $filter
    Description: Filters results.
    
    Name: $orderby
    Description: Currently supports lastMessagePreview/createdDateTime in descending order. Ascending order is currently not supported.

    Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/chat-list?view=graph-rest-1.0&tabs=http

_expand.description: Expand related entities. Currently supports 'members' and 'lastMessagePreview' properties. Example: 'members' or 'lastMessagePreview' or 'members,lastMessagePreview'

_top.description: Controls the number of items per response. Maximum allowed $top value is 50 per Microsoft Graph API specification.

_filter.description: Filters results. OData filter expression for filtering chat results.

_orderby.description: Sort order. Currently supports 'lastMessagePreview/createdDateTime desc' only. Ascending order is NOT supported per Microsoft Graph API. Example: 'lastMessagePreview/createdDateTime desc'

tools.name: list_chat_messages
tools.description:
List messages in a chat (Microsoft Graph API compliant).

    API Endpoint: GET /chats/{chat-id}/messages
    API Endpoint (with user context): GET /users/{user-id}/chats/{chat-id}/messages

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions:
    - Delegated (work or school account): Chat.Read (least privileged), Chat.ReadWrite (higher privileged)
      * Allows accessing YOUR OWN chats only (requires chat membership)
      * userId parameter is NOT required (uses authenticated user)
    - Application: ChatMessage.Read.Chat (least privileged), Chat.Read.All, Chat.ReadWrite.All (higher privileged)
      * Allows accessing ANY chat without membership requirement
      * userId parameter is REQUIRED to specify which user's context to operate in
      * Must use endpoint: /users/{user_id}/chats/{chat_id}/messages

    IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
    1. Delegated permissions (Chat.Read, Chat.ReadWrite):
       - User can ONLY list messages from their own chats (membership required)
       - userId parameter should NOT be provided
    2. Application permissions (ChatMessage.Read.Chat, Chat.Read.All, Chat.ReadWrite.All):
       - Can list messages from ANY chat without membership requirement
       - userId parameter is REQUIRED (to specify user context)
       - Returns 400 Bad Request if userId is missing

    Microsoft Graph API Query Parameters (EXACT from documentation):
    
    Name: $top
    - Controls the number of items per response
    - Maximum allowed $top value is 50
    
    Name: $orderby
    - Currently supports the lastModifiedDateTime (default) and createdDateTime properties in descending order
    - The ascending order is currently not supported
    - Valid values: "lastModifiedDateTime desc" (default), "createdDateTime desc"
    
    Name: $filter
    - Sets the date range filter for the lastModifiedDateTime and createdDateTime properties
    - The lastModifiedDateTime property supports the gt and lt operators
    - The createdDateTime property supports the lt operator
    - You can only filter results if the request URL contains the $orderby and $filter query parameters configured for the same property; otherwise, the $filter query option is ignored
    - Valid filter formats:
      * For lastModifiedDateTime: "lastModifiedDateTime gt {datetime}" or "lastModifiedDateTime lt {datetime}" or both with "and"
      * For createdDateTime: "createdDateTime lt {datetime}"
    
    Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/chat-list-messages?view=graph-rest-1.0&tabs=http

userId.description: User identifier (user ID or user principal name). REQUIRED for application permissions to specify which user's context to operate in. NOT required for delegated permissions (uses authenticated user automatically). Example: 'alice_manager' or 'alice.manager@techcorp.com'

_top.description: Controls the number of items per response. Maximum allowed value is 50 per Microsoft Graph API specification.

_orderby.description: Sort order. ONLY supports 'lastModifiedDateTime desc' (default) or 'createdDateTime desc'. Ascending order is NOT supported per Microsoft Graph API.

_filter.description: Date range filter. MUST match the property in _orderby parameter. Format: 'lastModifiedDateTime gt {ISO8601}' and/or 'lastModifiedDateTime lt {ISO8601}' OR 'createdDateTime lt {ISO8601}'. The lastModifiedDateTime property supports gt and lt operators. The createdDateTime property supports ONLY lt operator. Filter is IGNORED if _orderby and _filter use different properties. Examples: 'lastModifiedDateTime gt 2021-01-01T00:00:00.000Z and lastModifiedDateTime lt 2021-12-31T23:59:59.999Z' (when _orderby='lastModifiedDateTime desc'), 'createdDateTime lt 2021-12-31T23:59:59.999Z' (when _orderby='createdDateTime desc')

tools.name: add_tab_to_chat
tools.description:
Add (pin) a tab to the specified chat.

    Creates a new tab in a chat following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 structure.
    The corresponding app must already be installed in the chat.
    This endpoint corresponds to POST /chats/{chat-id}/tabs in Microsoft Graph API.
    Provide teamsApp_odata_bind; it may be a full URL, short 'teamsApps/{id}', or a bare app ID.

    API Endpoint: POST /chats/{chat_id}/tabs

chatId.description: Unique chat identifier where the tab will be added

teamsApp_odata_bind.description: Teams app reference. Must be full Microsoft Graph URL: https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/appCatalogs/teamsApps/{app-id}

configuration.description: Container for custom settings applied to a tab

entityId.description: Identifier for the entity hosted by the tab provider

contentUrl.description: URL used for rendering tab contents in Teams

removeUrl.description: URL called by Teams client when tab is removed

websiteUrl.description: URL for showing tab contents outside of Teams

tools.name: send_chat_message
tools.description:
Send a new message in a chat (Microsoft Graph API compliant). API Endpoint: POST /chats/{chat_id}/messages.

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):
    Permission type | Least privileged permissions | Higher privileged permissions
    Delegated (work or school account) | ChatMessage.Send | Chat.ReadWrite, Group.ReadWrite.All
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported | Not supported
    Application | Teamwork.Migrate.All | Not available

    Important: "This API can't create a new chat; you must use the list chats method to retrieve the ID of an existing chat before you can create a chat message."

    Terms of use: "It is a violation of the terms of use to use Microsoft Teams as a log file. Only send messages that people will read.

body.description: Message body content (required). Plaintext/HTML representation of the content of the chat message. Representation is specified by the contentType inside the body. The content is always in HTML if the chat message contains a chatMessageMention.

replyToId.description: Read-only. ID of the parent chat message or root chat message of the thread. (Only applies to chat messages in channels, not chats.)

subject.description: Subject of the message, in plaintext (optional)

summary.description: Summary text of the chat message that could be used for push notifications and summary views or fall back views. Only applies to channel chat messages, not chat messages in a chat.

importance.description: The importance of the chat message. The possible values are: normal, high, urgent.

locale.description: Locale of the chat message set by the client. Always set to en-us.

attachments.description: References to attached objects like files, tabs, meetings etc.

id.description: Read-only. Unique id of the attachment.

contentType.description: The media type of the content attachment. It can have the following values: reference, file, card.

contentUrl.description: URL for the content of the attachment. Supported protocols: http, https, file and data.

content.description: The content of the attachment. If the attachment is a rich card, set the property to the rich card object. This property and contentUrl are mutually exclusive.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image that the channel can use if it supports using an alternative, smaller form of content or contentUrl. For example, if you set contentType to application/word and set contentUrl to the location of the Word document, you might include a thumbnail image that represents the document. The channel could display the thumbnail image instead of the document. When the user clicks the image, the channel would open the document.

teamsAppId.description: The ID of the Teams app that is associated with the attachment. The property is used to attribute a Teams message card to the specified app.

mentions.description: List of entities mentioned in the chat message. Supported entities are: user, bot, team, channel, chat, and tag.

id.description: Index of an entity being mentioned in the specified chatMessage. Matches the {index} value in the corresponding <at id="{index}"> tag in the message body.

mentionText.description: String used to represent the mention. For example, a user's display name, a team name.

mentioned.description: The entity (user, application, team, or channel) that was @mentioned.

user.description: If a user is mentioned, this property contains the user details.

userIdentityType.description: Type of user identity. The possible values are: aadUser, onPremiseAadUser, anonymousGuest, federatedUser, personalMicrosoftAccountUser, skypeUser, phoneUser, unknownFutureValue and emailUser.

application.description: If an application is mentioned, this property contains the application details.

conversation.description: If a conversation (team/channel) is mentioned, this property contains the conversation details.

channelIdentity.description: If the message was sent in a channel, represents identity of the channel. This property is null for chat messages.

teamId.description: The identity of the team in which the channel is located.

channelId.description: The identity of the channel in which the message was posted.

policyViolation.description: Defines the properties of a policy violation set by a data loss prevention (DLP) application.

dlpAction.description: The action taken by the DLP provider on the message. Supported values are: none, notifySender, blockAccess, blockAccessExternal.

justificationText.description: Justification text provided by the sender of the message when overriding a policy violation.

policyTip.description: Information to display to the message sender about why the message was flagged as a violation.

userAction.description: Indicates the action taken by the user on a message blocked by the DLP provider. Supported values are: none, override, reportFalsePositive.

verdictDetails.description: Indicates what actions the sender may take in response to the policy violation. Supported values are: none, allowFalsePositiveOverride, allowOverrideWithoutJustification, allowOverrideWithJustification.

messageType.description: The type of chat message. The possible values are: message, chatEvent, typing, unknownFutureValue, systemEventMessage.

tools.name: soft_delete_chat_message
tools.description:
Delete a single chatMessage in a chat (Microsoft Graph API compliant). API Endpoint: POST /users/{userId}/chats/{chatsId}/messages/{chatMessageId}/softDelete.

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):
    Permission type | Permissions (from least to most privileged)
    Delegated (work or school account) | Chat.ReadWrite
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported
    Application | Not supported

    Important Constraints:
    - Don't supply a request body for this method
    - Only delegated permissions are supported (application permissions NOT supported)
    - Personal Microsoft accounts are not supported
    - This API works differently in one or more national clouds
    - Available in all national cloud deployments: Global service , US Government L4 , US Government L5 (DOD) , China operated by 21Vianet

    Response: 204 No Content on success

    Note: This implements the Microsoft Graph API endpoint /users/{userId}/chats/{chatsId}/messages/{chatMessageId}/softDelete

messageId.description: Unique message identifier to soft delete

tools.name: undo_soft_delete_chat_message
tools.description:
Undo soft deletion of a single chatMessage in a chat (Microsoft Graph API compliant). API Endpoint: POST /users/{userId}/chats/{chatsId}/messages/{chatMessageId}/undoSoftDelete.

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):
    Permission type | Permissions (from least to most privileged)
    Delegated (work or school account) | Chat.ReadWrite
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported
    Application | Not supported

    Important Constraints:
    - Don't supply a request body for this method
    - Only delegated permissions are supported (application permissions NOT supported)
    - Personal Microsoft accounts are not supported
    - Available in all national cloud deployments: Global service , US Government L4 , US Government L5 (DOD) , China operated by 21Vianet

    Response: 204 No Content on success

    Note: This implements the Microsoft Graph API endpoint /users/{userId}/chats/{chatsId}/messages/{chatMessageId}/undoSoftDelete

tools.name: update_chat_message
tools.description:
Update a chat message (Microsoft Graph API compliant). API Endpoint: PATCH /chats/{chat_id}/messages/{message_id}.

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):
    Permission type | Permissions (from least to most privileged)
    Delegated (work or school account) | Chat.ReadWrite
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported
    Application | Chat.UpdatePolicyViolation.All, Chat.ReadWrite.All

    Important Constraints:
    - Delegated permissions: ALL properties can be updated EXCEPT policyViolation
    - Application permissions: ONLY policyViolation property can be updated (Chat.UpdatePolicyViolation.All) OR all properties can be updated (Chat.ReadWrite.All)
    - Only works for chats where members are Microsoft Teams users (if Skype user present, operation fails)
    - Only the user who sent the message can perform updates
    - This method doesn't support federation
    - When used with application permissions, this API is metered and supports model=A payment model

    Response Codes: Delegated permissions (204 No Content), Application permissions (200 OK for policyViolation updates)

importance.description: Updated importance level of the message

mentions.description: Updated user mentions in the message

policyViolation.description: ONLY application permissions (Chat.UpdatePolicyViolation.All) can update this property. Defines the properties of a policy violation set by a data loss prevention (DLP) application.

dlpAction.description: The action taken by the DLP provider on the message. Supported values are: none, notifySender, blockAccess, blockAccessExternal.

tools.name: list_all_channels

Get the list of channels either in this team or shared with this team (Microsoft Graph API compliant).

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):

    Delegated (work or school account) | Channel.ReadBasic.All | ChannelSettings.Read.All, ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported | Not supported
    Application | Channel.ReadBasic.All | ChannelSettings.Read.All, ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All

    Permission-Based Field Restrictions:
    - Channel.ReadBasic.All: Returns ONLY displayName and description fields (per Microsoft Graph API spec: "Read the names and descriptions of all channels")
    - ChannelSettings.Read.All or higher: Returns all channel properties including id, createdDateTime, membershipType, tenantId, isArchived, etc.
    - Response always includes @odata.id property for Microsoft Graph API compliance

    Important Features:
    - This API supports admin permissions. Microsoft Teams service admins can access teams that they are not a member of
    - Use _select to exclude the email and moderationSettings properties for better performance
    - Supports _filter for membershipType (e.g., 'membershipType eq shared', 'membershipType eq private', 'membershipType eq standard')
    - Response includes @odata.id property which can be used to access the channel
    - Lists all channels in the team and shared channels from other teams

    Sample Requests:
    - All channels: GET /teams/{team-id}/allChannels
    - Only shared channels: GET /teams/{team-id}/allChannels?_filter=membershipType eq 'shared'
    - Performance optimized: GET /teams/{team-id}/allChannels?_select=displayName,membershipType
    - Basic permission (Channel.ReadBasic.All): Returns only displayName and description automatically

    Available in all national cloud deployments: Global service , US Government L4 , US Government L5 (DOD) , China operated by 21Vianet

tools.name: update_channel_message
tools.description:
Update a chatMessage in a channel (Microsoft Graph API compliant).

    ONLY 3 FIELDS ARE REQUIRED:
    1. teamId (REQUIRED)
    2. channelId (REQUIRED)
    3. messageId (REQUIRED)

    ALL OTHER FIELDS ARE OPTIONAL - Only provide fields you want to update!
    DO NOT send fields with empty values!

    Example: To update only message content, send ONLY teamId, channelId, messageId, and body with non-empty content.

    API Endpoints:
    - PATCH /teams/{team-id}/channels/{channel-id}/messages/{message-id}
    - PATCH /teams/{team-id}/channels/{channel-id}/messages/{message-id}/replies/{reply-id}

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):

    Permissions for channel:
    Permission type | Permissions (from least to most privileged)
    Delegated (work or school account) | ChannelMessage.ReadWrite, Group.ReadWrite.All
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported
    Application | ChannelMessage.UpdatePolicyViolation.All, Group.ReadWrite.All

    Important Constraints:
    - The Group.ReadWrite.All permission is supported only for backward compatibility (update solutions to use alternative permissions)
    - For delegated permissions: ALL properties of a chatMessage can be updated EXCEPT the policyViolation property
    - For application permissions: ONLY the policyViolation property can be updated
    - The update only works for chats where members are Microsoft Teams users. If one participant is using Skype, the operation fails
    - This method doesn't support federation. Only the user in the tenant who sent the message can perform data loss prevention (DLP) updates on the specified chat message
    - When used with application permissions, this API is metered. It supports the model=A payment model. For details, see Payment models. If you don't specify a payment model in your query, the default evaluation mode will be used
    - Available in national cloud deployments: Global service , US Government L4 , US Government L5 (DOD) , China operated by 21Vianet

    Response:
    - For delegated permissions: 204 No Content
    - For application permissions: 200 OK

tools.description:
Update the properties of a tab in a channel (Microsoft Graph API compliant).

    API Endpoint: PATCH /teams/{team-id}/channels/{channel-id}/tabs/{tab-id}

    Microsoft Graph API Permissions (exact from documentation):

    Permission type | Least privileged permissions | Higher privileged permissions
    Delegated (work or school account) | TeamsTab.ReadWriteSelfForTeam | Directory.ReadWrite.All, Group.ReadWrite.All, TeamsTab.ReadWrite.All, TeamsTab.ReadWriteForTeam
    Delegated (personal Microsoft account) | Not supported | Not supported
    Application | TeamsTab.ReadWrite.Group | Directory.ReadWrite.All, Group.ReadWrite.All, TeamsTab.ReadWrite.All, TeamsTab.ReadWriteForTeam.All, TeamsTab.ReadWriteSelfForTeam.All

    Important Notes:
    - The TeamsTab.ReadWrite.Group permission uses resource-specific consent
    - The Directory and Group permissions are supported only for backward compatibility
    - Only displayName and configuration properties can be updated
    - Attempting to update read-only fields (id, webUrl, sortOrderIndex, messageId, teamsAppId, teamsApp) returns 400 Bad Request

    Updatable Properties ONLY:
    - displayName (string, 1-128 characters)
    - configuration (object)

    Read-Only Properties (cannot update):
    - id, webUrl, sortOrderIndex, messageId, teamsAppId, teamsApp

    Response: 200 OK with updated tab object

    Available in national cloud deployments: Global service , US Government L4 , US Government L5 (DOD) , China operated by 21Vianet

tools.description: Create a new group following MS Graph API v1.0. Creates a Microsoft Entra group which can be a Microsoft 365 group, a security group, or a distribution group. securityEnabled.description: REQUIRED. Set to true for security-enabled groups, including Microsoft 365 groups. Required. Note: Groups created using the Microsoft Entra admin center or the Azure portal always have securityEnabled initially set to true.

description.description: OPTIONAL. An optional description for the group.

groupTypes.description: OPTIONAL. Specifies the group type and its membership. If the collection contains 'Unified', the group is a Microsoft 365 group; otherwise, it's either a security group or a distribution group. If the collection includes 'DynamicMembership', the group has dynamic membership; otherwise, membership is static.

visibility.description: OPTIONAL. Specifies the group join policy and group content visibility for groups. Possible values are: Private, Public, or HiddenMembership. HiddenMembership can be set only for Microsoft 365 groups when the groups are created. It can't be updated later.

classification.description: OPTIONAL. Describes a classification for the group (such as low, medium or high business impact). Valid values for this property are defined by creating a ClassificationList setting value, based on the template definition.

isAssignableToRole.description: OPTIONAL. Indicates whether this group can be assigned to a Microsoft Entra role. Optional. This property can only be set while creating the group and is immutable. If set to true, the securityEnabled property must also be set to true, visibility must be Hidden, and the group cannot be a dynamic group (that is, groupTypes cannot contain DynamicMembership). Only callers with at least the Privileged Role Administrator role can set this property. The caller must also be assigned the RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory permission to set this property or update the membership of such groups.

Indicates whether the group is a member of a restricted management administrative unit, in which case it requires a role scoped to the restricted administrative unit to manage. tools.name: list_call_records
tools.description:
Get the list of callRecord objects and their properties.

        API Endpoint: GET /communications/callRecords

        Permissions (Application ONLY - Delegated NOT supported):
        - CallRecords.Read.All (required)

        Important Notes:
        - Delegated permissions are NOT supported - only application permissions allowed
        - A call record is created after a call or meeting ends and remains available for 30 days
        - This API doesn't return call records older than 30 days
        - Listed call records don't include expandable relationships (sessions, participants_v2)
        - Default page size: 60 entries
        - Admin consent required

        Response includes:
        - @odata.context: OData context URL
        - @odata.nextLink: URL for next page if more results available
        - value: Array of callRecord objects with:
          - id: Unique identifier
          - version: Version number (typically 2)
          - type: Type of call (unknown, groupCall, peerToPeer)
          - modalities: List of modalities (audio, video, videoBasedScreenSharing, data)
          - lastModifiedDateTime: Last modified timestamp
          - startDateTime: Call start timestamp
          - endDateTime: Call end timestamp
          - organizer: Organizer identity information
          - organizer_v2: Newer format organizer with full identity

        Available in national cloud deployments:
        - Global service
        - US Government L4
        - US Government L5 (DOD)
        - China operated by 21Vianet

        Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/callrecords-cloudcommunications-list-callrecords

_filter.description:
Filter query parameter. Supported filters:

                    1. startDateTime (supports: lt, le, gt, ge)
                       Example: "startDateTime ge 2023-09-25T09:25:00Z and startDateTime lt 2023-09-25T09:30:00Z"

                    2. participants_v2.id (supports: eq)
                       Example: "participants_v2/any(p:p/id eq '821809f5-0000-0000-0000-3b5136c0e777')"

                    Note: Only these two filter properties are supported.

Select specific properties to return.

                    Available properties: id, version, type, modalities, lastModifiedDateTime, startDateTime, endDateTime, organizer, organizer_v2

tools.name: get_call_record

        API Endpoint: GET /communications/callRecords/{id}

        Permissions (Application ONLY - Delegated NOT supported):
        - CallRecords.Read.All (required)

        Important Notes:
        - Delegated permissions are NOT supported - only application permissions allowed
        - A call record is created after a call or meeting ends and remains available for 30 days
        - This API doesn't return call records older than 30 days
        - Requests for records older than 30 days result in 404 Not Found
        - Admin consent required

        How to get call record ID:
        1. Subscribe to change notifications to /communications/callRecords endpoint
        2. Use the callChainId property of a call (record available after call completes)

        Query Parameters:
        - _select: Return specific properties (e.g., "id,startDateTime,endDateTime,modalities")
        - _expand: Include relationships:
          - participants_v2: Expand full participant list (max 130 entries per page)
          - sessions(_expand=segments): Expand sessions with their segments (max 60 sessions per page)

        Response includes:
        - id: Unique identifier
        - version: Version number
        - type: Call type (peerToPeer, groupCall, unknown)
        - modalities: Array of modalities (audio, video, videoBasedScreenSharing, data)
        - lastModifiedDateTime: Last modified timestamp
        - startDateTime: Call start timestamp
        - endDateTime: Call end timestamp
        - organizer_v2: Organizer identity with full details
        - participants_v2: Array of participants (when expanded)
        - sessions: Array of session objects (when expanded)
        Request Headers (optional):
        - Prefer: odata.maxpagesize={x} - Preferred page size for paginated results
        - Prefer: include-unknown-enum-members - Enable evolvable enum values
        - Prefer: omit-values=nulls - Remove null/empty values from response
        Available in national cloud deployments:
        - Global service
        - US Government L4
        - US Government L5 (DOD)
        - China operated by 21Vianet
        Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/callrecords-callrecord-get

Select specific properties to return.

                    Example: "id,startDateTime,endDateTime,modalities"

                    Note: Only supported for callRecord and session resources.

Expand relationships to include in the response.

tools.name: create_call
tools.description:
Create a new outgoing peer-to-peer or group call, or join an existing meeting.

Supported scenarios:
- Peer-to-peer VoIP calls (service or app-hosted media)
- Group calls with up to 5 VoIP users
- PSTN calls (requires application instance with PSTN number)
- Join scheduled meetings (using chatInfo and meetingInfo)
- Join meetings with joinMeetingId and optional passcode

Permissions (Application only):
- Calls.JoinGroupCalls.Chat (least privileged for joining meetings)
- Calls.Initiate.All (for peer-to-peer calls)
- Calls.InitiateGroupCall.All (for group calls)
- Calls.JoinGroupCall.All (for joining meetings)
- Calls.JoinGroupCallAsGuest.All
- Calls.AccessMedia.All (required for app-hosted media)

Note: Delegated permissions are not supported for this API.

source.description: Source participant info (optional, for PSTN calls with applicationInstance)

targets.description: Target participants for peer-to-peer or group calls (max 5). Do not use with meetingInfo.

chatInfo.description: Chat information for joining meetings (use with meetingInfo)

meetingInfo.description: Meeting information for joining meetings. Do not use with targets.

tools.name: create_audio_routing_group
tools.description:
Create a new audio routing group for a call.

The audio routing group stores a private audio route between participants in a
multiparty conversation. Source is the participant itself and the receivers are
a subset of other participants in the multiparty conversation.

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- **PERMISSION REQUIRED**: Application-only permission (NO delegated)
  - Calls.JoinGroupCall.All (least privileged)
  - Calls.InitiateGroupCall.All (higher privileged)
- **4-speaker limit**: Only top 4 dominant speakers' audio forwarded in group calls
- If speaker not loud enough in main mixer, bot won't hear even with private audio group

ROUTING MODES:
1. **oneToOne** (1 source ↔ 1 receiver):
   - Immutable after creation (cannot be updated)
   - Exactly 1 source participant required
   - Exactly 1 receiver participant required
   - For private 1-to-1 audio channel

2. **multicast** (1 source → multiple receivers):
   - Source has 1 participant
   - Multiple receivers allowed
   - Receiver list can be updated later
   - Useful for broadcast scenarios

CRITICAL VALIDATION RULES (Per Microsoft Graph API):
- Sources must have exactly 1 participant ID (the authenticated bot/application)
- Source participant MUST be the authenticated bot making the API call
- Receivers must have at least 1 participant ID
- Routing mode MUST match participant count:
  * 1 source + 1 receiver → MUST use "oneToOne" mode
  * 1 source + 2+ receivers → MUST use "multicast" mode
- Parent call must be in active state (not terminated/terminating)
- All participants must exist in the call and belong to same organization

Permissions (Application only):
- Calls.JoinGroupCall.All (least privileged)
- Calls.InitiateGroupCall.All (higher privileged)

Note: Delegated permissions are NOT supported.

routingMode.description: Routing mode: oneToOne (immutable) or multicast (updatable receivers)

sources.description: Source participant IDs (must have exactly 1 participant - the authenticated bot/application making this API call)

receivers.description: Receiver participant IDs. For oneToOne mode: exactly 1 receiver. For multicast mode: 2 or more receivers.

tools.name: set_user_status_message
tools.description:
Set a user's status message.

        Sets the state of a user's status message following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 structure.
        This endpoint corresponds to POST /users/{id}/presence/setStatusMessage in Microsoft Graph API.

        The status message is displayed in Microsoft Teams and other applications that support
        presence information. This provides users with a way to communicate additional context
        about their current status beyond just availability and activity.

        Request Parameters:
          - user_id: Required. User identifier - accepts either user ID (UUID) or userPrincipalName
          - statusMessage: Required. Object containing status message details
            * message: Required. The status message text (maximum 280 characters)
            * expiryDateTime: Optional. Object containing dateTime and timeZone when the status message expires

        Status Message Behavior:
          - Displayed across Microsoft Teams and other presence-aware applications
          - If no expiry time is set, the message persists until manually cleared
          - Maximum message length is 280 characters (same as Microsoft Teams)
          - Empty or null message will clear the existing status message
          - Status message is independent of presence availability and activity

        Use Cases:
          - "In a meeting until 3pm"
          - "Working from home today"
          - "Back in 30 minutes"
          - "Focus time - please use email"
          - "On vacation until next Monday"

        Response:
          - Returns 200 OK with empty response body on successful operation
          - Follows Microsoft Graph API v1.0 response format

        Authentication:
          - Requires x-teams-access-token header with valid access token
          - Token will be validated against user database

        Permissions Required:
          - Presence.ReadWrite:delegated (can set own status message)
          - Presence.ReadWrite.All:application (can set any user's status message)

        Status Codes:
          - 200: Success - Status message set successfully
          - 400: Bad Request - Invalid message or malformed request
          - 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing access token
          - 403: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions
          - 404: Not Found - User not found
          - 500: Internal Server Error

        Microsoft Graph Documentation:
        https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/presence-setstatusmessage

user_id.description: User identifier - accepts either user ID (UUID) or userPrincipalName (e.g., 'alice_manager')

statusMessage.description: Status message object containing message details

message.description: Message object with content and contentType

expiryDateTime.description: Expiry date and time information for the status message

dateTime.description: The date and time in ISO 8601 format

timeZone.description: The time zone of the dateTime

        Application permissions are explicitly not supported for this API.

        Request Body Properties (all following Microsoft Graph API v1.0 spec):
        - displayName (required): The display name of the webinar (1-500 characters)
        - description (required): A description with:
          - contentType: 'text' or 'html'
          - content: The description text
        - startDateTime (required): Start date-time with:
          - dateTime: ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2024-03-30T10:00:00')
          - timeZone: Timezone name (e.g., 'Pacific Standard Time')
        - endDateTime (required): End date-time (must be after startDateTime) with:
          - dateTime: ISO 8601 format
          - timeZone: Timezone name
        - audience (required): Webinar visibility
          - Possible values: 'everyone', 'organization', 'unknownFutureValue'
        - coOrganizers (optional): Array of user identities, each with:
          - id: User ID (required) - displayName and tenantId are auto-populated from database
        - settings (optional): Webinar settings with:
          - isAttendeeEmailNotificationEnabled: Boolean (default: false)

        Response Properties:
        - id: Unique identifier (format: guid@guid)
        - status: Webinar status (always 'draft' on creation)

          - device: null

        - settings: Webinar settings
        - externalEventInformation: Empty array initially

        - Webinars are always created in 'draft' status
        - Only delegated permissions supported (no application permissions)
        - Personal Microsoft accounts are NOT supported
        - endDateTime must be after startDateTime
        - CoOrganizers must exist in the system
        - Creator information is auto-populated from authenticated user

        Available in national cloud deployments:
        - Global service 
        - US Government L4 
        - US Government L5 (DOD) 
        - China operated by 21Vianet (status unknown)

        Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/virtualeventsroot-post-webinars

tools.name: publish_virtual_event_webinar
tools.description:
Publish a virtualEventWebinar to make it visible to its audience.
        
        CRITICAL: Only the Organizer (creator) can publish a webinar.
        Co-organizers CANNOT publish (unlike the UPDATE API).
        
        **Action:** Changes webinar status from 'draft' to 'published'
        
        **Status Transitions:**
        - draft → published (allowed, returns 204 No Content)
        - published → published (idempotent, returns 204 No Content)
        - canceled → published (error: cannot publish canceled webinar, returns 400)
        
        **Permissions Required:**
        - VirtualEvent.ReadWrite:delegated (only delegated, NOT application)
        
        **Response:** 204 No Content on success (no response body)
        
        **Authorization:**
        - ONLY the Organizer can publish
        - Co-organizers are NOT allowed to publish
        - Non-organizers will receive 403 Forbidden
        
        Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/virtualeventwebinar-publish

          - dateTime: ISO 8601 format

          - Can ONLY be true if audience='organization'

          - tenantId: Tenant ID (required) - displayName auto-populated from database

        - createdBy: Creator identity information (auto-populated from authenticated user)
          - application: null
          - device: null
          - user: User identity with @odata.type, id, displayName, tenantId
        - coOrganizers: Array of coorganizer identities (with displayName populated)
        - settings: Townhall settings
        - externalEventInformation: Empty array initially

        Important Notes:
        - Townhalls are always created in 'draft' status
        - Only delegated permissions supported (no application permissions)
        - Personal Microsoft accounts are NOT supported
        - endDateTime must be after startDateTime
        - isInviteOnly can ONLY be true if audience='organization'
        - CoOrganizers must exist in the system
        - Invited attendees can be users (must exist) or guests (validated by email)
        - Creator information is auto-populated from authenticated user

Cancel a virtualEventTownhall. A canceled town hall has its status set to canceled permanently.
        
        CRITICAL: Only the Organizer (creator) can cancel a townhall.
        Co-organizers CANNOT cancel.
        
        **Action:** Changes townhall status to 'canceled' permanently
        
        **Status Transitions:**
        - draft → canceled (allowed, returns 204 No Content)
        - published → canceled (allowed, returns 204 No Content)
        - canceled → canceled (idempotent, returns 200 OK with message)
        
        **Permissions Required:**
        - VirtualEvent.ReadWrite:delegated (only delegated, NOT application)
        
        **Response:** 204 No Content on success (no response body)
        
        **Authorization:**
        - ONLY the Organizer can cancel
        - Co-organizers are NOT allowed to cancel
        - Non-organizers will receive 403 Forbidden
        
        **Note:** You can still get a canceled town hall by using the Get virtualEventTownhall API.
        
        **API Endpoint:** POST /solutions/virtualEvents/townhalls/{townhallId}/cancel
        
        Permissions (EXACT from Microsoft Documentation):
        - Delegated (work or school account): VirtualEvent.ReadWrite (least privileged)
        - Delegated (personal Microsoft account): NOT SUPPORTED
        - Application: NOT SUPPORTED
        
        Request Parameters:
        - townhall_id (required): The unique identifier of the townhall (format: guid@guid)
        
        Response:
        - 204 No Content: Townhall successfully canceled
        - 200 OK: Townhall already canceled (idempotent)
        - 404 Not Found: Townhall doesn't exist

tools.name: list_teams_apps
tools.description:
List Teams apps from the Microsoft Teams app catalog.

API Endpoint: GET /appCatalogs/teamsApps

This tool lists all Teams apps available in the organization's app catalog. You can filter, select specific fields, and expand related properties using OData query parameters.

Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/appcatalogs-list-teamsapps

Common Use Cases:
1. List all organization-specific apps: Use _filter=distributionMethod eq 'organization'
2. Find app by ID: Use _filter=id eq '{app-id}'
3. Find app by external ID (manifest ID): Use _filter=externalId eq '{manifest-id}'
4. Get specific fields only: Use _select=id,displayName,distributionMethod

Required Permissions:
- AppCatalog.Read.All (least privileged)
- AppCatalog.ReadWrite.All
- Directory.Read.All

_filter.description: OData filter to apply. Examples: "distributionMethod eq 'organization'", "id eq 'b1c5353a-7aca-41b3-830f-27d5218fe0e5'", "externalId eq 'cf1ba4c7-f94e-4d80-ba90-5594b641a8ee'"

_select.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return. Example: "id,displayName,distributionMethod"

_expand.description: Comma-separated list of relationships to expand. Example: "appDefinitions"
